lessons.trigger: opportunity density > capital → per-venue adaptive sizing. Good opportunities concentrate on a few capital-consumption points (gateio/bithumb buys, the $19 KRW pool, the $75 gate-futures hedge pool) while the fund as a whole sits idle; a global average hides the local starvation.
lessons.problem: A single fund-wide size/threshold cannot express that capital is scarce at ONE venue and slack everywhere else; averaging pressure makes a real gateio/KRW/gate-futures bottleneck invisible and spends the scarcest capital on marginal 0.5% edges.
lessons.change: New shared layer nw_dynamic_sizing.py (NW_DYN_*): per pass, per capital-consumption point, pressure = Σ(target size)÷available capital drives a 2-stage staircase — raise net threshold 0.5→0.8% first, then lower max size $300→$210 (floor −30%, min $40 fixed). Reverse-ladder relaxation; asymmetric hysteresis (fast escalate, slow relax). Only bottlenecked points tighten.
lessons.expected_effect: Higher risk-adjusted return per unit of the SAME $10k fund: scarce capital buys 0.8% A-grade edges, only over-subscribed A-grade points diversify smaller. assumptions.dyn stamps (threshold,max,pressure,stage,point,cap_source) for Sharpe-by-regime attribution; capital_short logs the fund-increase evidence. First live passes: $75 gate-futures + $19 KRW pools hit the floor while the $300 bybit pool (pressure 4) held at 0.8%/$300 — the per-venue differentiation the design is for.

lessons.trigger: strategy #4 capital plan — wallet-type pools. Covered Arb needs more than spot working capital: it locks collateral for margin shorts and margin for perp hedges, and Robin is adding an external ₩7.5M Korean lending deposit. The Quartermaster Planner (shadow) modelled only one fungible spot pool per venue, so it could not tell locked hedge capital from transferable working capital, nor plan how that capital gets seated.
lessons.problem: A single-pool-per-venue capital model double-counts locked capital: reserving $300 UTA collateral at bybit or $75 futures float at gate leaves that money unavailable for spot working capital, but the floor/cap planner still counted it as spendable. There was also no plan artifact for HOW a hedge pool is seated (bybit UTA has $0 → capital must move venues; gate is a classic account → an internal spot→futures wallet move) and no verification the hedge products exist or that the funding route's USDT dep/wd is open.
lessons.change: Quartermaster v0.2 (nw_quartermaster_v0.py). Wallet-type reservations: hedge_uta (bybit UTA, unified margin-short collateral + perp margin, $300), hedge_futures (gate futures float $75 via internal spot→futures wallet_move), lending_collateral (bithumb ₩7.5M EXTERNAL, funded=false/source=external, not carved from the $10k). Floor interaction subtracts locked hedge USD from available working capital at the venue where it is drawn (gate charges itself $75; bybit has $0 so its $300 is seated by a verified cross-venue funding move from binance, which is charged) — no double counting. Product verification before reserving via live public APIs (plan.verification): bybit linear perps + spot-margin/UTA (marginTrading flag, verified_via=docs+instruments), gate futures + margin pairs; aux falls back to binance futures on failure. DW route verification annotates route_verified + chain on every inter-venue move from the USDT transfer map; bithumb inflow gets a fiat-wire note + USDT-rail alternate. Plan gains reservations[]/wallet_moves[]/verification{}/capital_model{}; version 0.1→0.2. Rides the existing /arb/thusus/fund passthrough; no stats.py change. Still shadow/paper.
lessons.expected_effect: Effective working-capital balances = raw minus locked_by_venue ({binance:300, gate:75} in the first pass), so floor deficits/surpluses use money that can actually move. Each hedge pool carries a concrete seating plan + live product-verification stamp; the external ₩7.5M is a distinct locked pool that never inflates the fund. First live pass (2026-07-17 18:24 UTC): hub bybit verified (743 perps, 263 UTA-margin), aux gate verified (835 futures, 735 margin), 3 reservations, hedge_reserved_usd=375, external_collateral≈$5049 (fx 1485), 1 funding + 1 wallet move, route_verification all_verified=true. Reservation sizes get tuned from the hedged engine's measured concurrent-hedge pace.

lessons.trigger: 39% livescan capture + instrument-selection design — livescan keeps only ~39% of quoted edge because the 15-60min transit window is unhedged (measured 61% decay, 121-trade sample). Robin: hedge the arb at the RICH venue so the edge is locked at t=0, and pick the hedge instrument per trade.
lessons.problem: An unhedged transfer arb carries its full price risk across the transit window so most quoted edge evaporates before arrival, and there was no per-trade choice of HOW to hedge (margin short vs perp short differ in cost + coverage, and the cheaper flips by token) nor any proof of how much hedging recovers.
lessons.change: New shadow engine nw_hedged_shadow.py (strategy #4, Covered Arb). Picks a hedge at the RICH venue by min INCREMENTAL cost: margin short (interest only — spot sell fee is not incremental; gate APR live, others assumed; preference bonus) or perp short (2x taker + sign-aware funding, per-contract interval; never mexc/bitget); neither -> unhedged-comparable skip. Perp contract quantization -> hedge_coverage_pct (perp <100% or 0 below min contract; <70% loses to margin). Reuses /arb/board + /arb/kimchi_roundturn feeds and the per-chain settle clock. Records the A/B: capture_hedged vs capture_unhedged. Execution-protocol subset: T-0 entry stamps, in-flight invariant, exit modes E1/E2/E5 + E3/E4/E6 scenario costs. Own table nw_hedged_shadow; joined into /thusus as strategy 'hedged' + GET /arb/hedged_shadow. Capital model shadow-only.
lessons.expected_effect: Hedged capture should sit far above the ~39% unhedged baseline (target >=85%); the per-trade A/B is the evidence. Cost hierarchy is NOT fixed (majors -> margin cheapest ~0.002%/4h; hot alts borrow-spike -> perp wins; bithumb daily-ceil priciest) so the comparison is live per trade. The $40-300 band makes perp min-contract/min-notional bite often on majors -> many perp candidates cover <70% and correctly lose to margin. First passes are thin; the A/B accumulates.

lessons.trigger: flat +5min settle overstated ETH-route P&L (Robin: ETH deposit takes AT LEAST 5min, so the full withdraw->confirm->credit transfer is well over 5min, realistically 10-20min). The paper engine settled EVERY chain at a flat +5:00, so slow-chain rows were marked against a too-early tick.
lessons.problem: The settle-timing model ignored per-chain transfer latency. Settling an Ethereum-mainnet route against the +5min book let it absorb LESS spread decay than the coins really experience in transit, so slow-chain realized P&L looked more profitable than reality. Ethereum is the #2 chain in the data (~10 trades), so the bug materially inflated ETH-route capture.
lessons.change: Per-chain settle delay in nw_paper_arb.py (shared by big-spike): chain_settle_delay(route_chain) -> minutes. ETH mainnet 15min (NW_PAPER_SETTLE_DELAY_ETH_MIN=15, NOT instant); fast chains/L2s + TRON keep 5min (NW_PAPER_SETTLE_DELAY_MIN=5); unknown chains default 5 and are logged once. Acceptance window = delay + NW_PAPER_SETTLE_BUFFER_MIN (3), so fast=8min (unchanged), ETH=18min. Whole map overridable via NW_PAPER_SETTLE_DELAY_MAP (JSON). Per-row settle timing, the pending-settle loop, and the dynamic sleep all use the row's chain delay; big-spike inherits via import. PLUS exposure-time-adjusted sizing: ETH-mainnet routes capped at $150 max (half) — exposure-time-adjusted sizing via NW_PAPER_SETTLE_ETH_MAX_SIZE_USD=150, clamped in the size search on the same ETH classifier (MIN stays $40; skip honestly if no feasible size >= $40). Livescan + big-spike; woncarry untouched. Forward-only: historical rows untouched.
lessons.expected_effect: ETH-route rows settle against the +15min tick, absorb more spread decay -> realized P&L drops to honest levels and more ETH routes fail the net gate. ETH-route position risk halved to match ~3x settle window (max $150 vs $300). Fast-chain behaviour identical (5min/8min window, $300 max). Capture ratio BY CHAIN becomes comparable, the calibration signal for tuning these initial-estimate delays with real latency data.

lessons.trigger: ~$500 Korean entries exploding + KRW pool overshoot: the won-carry KRW pool ran to 35.9% of the fund against a 30% cap, driven partly by oversized single entries (Korea's per-entry cap was $600 vs the global $350). Big trades were measured eroding their own capture 8-22% via footprint.
lessons.problem: Oversized single entries + single-name/route concentration erode a stable realized return. Korea sized entries almost 2x the global standard, so capital rushed into the KRW pool in big chunks and overshot its cap; and any one token or one (buy_ex,sell_ex) route could absorb an outsized share of the fund.
lessons.change: Uniform per-trade band across ALL engines: MIN $40, MAX $300 (NW_PAPER_SIZE_MIN_USD 20->40, NW_PAPER_SIZE_MAX_USD 350->300; NW_WONCARRY_MAX_USD 600->300, NW_WONCARRY_SIZE_GRID_MIN 20->40; Render API NW_KIMCHI_ROUNDTURN_MAX_USD 600->300; big-spike inherits the band and keeps SIZE_FACTOR=0.5). Plus diversification guards NW_DIV_MAX_PER_TOKEN_USD=300 (open exposure per base across venues; extra entries shrink to headroom or skip) and NW_DIV_MAX_PER_PAIR=3 (max concurrent open positions per route), applied to all three engines, plus a breadth reorder (round-robin by base).
lessons.expected_effect: KRW inflow throttled to the global standard (no more ~$500 Korean chunks) so the KRW pool stops overshooting its 30% cap from oversized entries. Realized capture stabilises via breadth: no name holds more than $300 open and no route more than 3 concurrent positions, so big-footprint single trades are reduced. Matters more as parallel real-time scans surface more opportunities.

lessons.trigger: Robin's directive (2026-07-17): won-carry accumulates KRW but never deliberately exits it. Parked KRW carries FX risk and misses global opportunities; the naive exit (repurchase USDT) pays the full stablecoin premium. Exit instead through the most undervalued token on the Korean venue whenever its edge covers costs.
lessons.problem: KRW capital lock + USDT-premium exit cost class — the engine had in-legs (premium buys that accrue KRW) but no formalized out-leg. Repurchasing USDT to exit re-pays the stablecoin basis + fees, and idle KRW is unhedged FX exposure the accounting could not separate from carry P&L.
lessons.change: Exit-policy v2 params: NW_WC_EXIT_EDGE_MIN_PCT=0.0 (KRW-funded discount out-legs execute when executable exit_edge >= this; standalone MIN_NET floors waived); NW_WC_KRW_POOL_CAP_PCT=30 (premium in-legs blocked once KRW share exceeds this); NW_WC_MAX_DWELL_H=72 with NW_WC_FORCED_EXIT_MAJORS=BTC,ETH,XRP,SOL (parked-too-long KRW force-exits via the lowest-relative-premium liquid major, even at negative edge, logged as forced_exit); fx_pnl marks the KRW pool at current usdt_krw each pass so carry_pnl vs FX exposure are separable.
lessons.expected_effect: Out-legs execute only when covered (exit_edge >= 0 => at least as cheap as repurchasing USDT); in-legs stop over-parking KRW above 30%; forced exits bounded to the dwell window and liquid majors; fx_pnl separated from carry_pnl in every pass summary.

lessons.trigger: nw_woncarry_shadow — 0 rows across ~2156 passes; heartbeat shows opp_count 40, recheck_wait 37, entered 0 every pass
lessons.problem: An engine that evaluates candidates every pass but persists nothing when they all reject is unauditable — the fund cannot tell a correctly-empty strategy from a silently-broken gate.
lessons.change: NW_WC_CAND_LOG_SEC: (new) → 300 — added table nw_woncarry_candidates; top-10 candidates by |edge| persisted per throttled pass with the gate's own executable numbers + reject reason
lessons.expected_effect: Within ~10–15 min the table holds rows; exec_net_pct should be NEGATIVE for the headline candidates, proving the executable gate is correctly refusing sub-cost basis, not malfunctioning.

lessons.trigger: ROAM #7911 (kucoin→bitget, entry spread 7.03%, expected +$13.50 → realized −$8.61, a −$22.1 drift); ELIZAOS #7897 (gateio→bybit, spread 2.56%, expected +$8.32 → realized −$10.24). |drift| vs entry-spread correlation 0.485 across settled livescan trades.
lessons.problem: Wide entry spread predicts adverse 5-minute drift — a large touch-to-touch gap is priced by the market's uncertainty about the name, and entries above a spread threshold carry uncompensated reversal risk regardless of token.
lessons.change: Livescan NW_PAPER_MAX_SPREAD_PCT: off → 3.0. Sniper NW_BIGSPIKE_MAX_SPREAD_PCT: off → 10.0 (DELIBERATE deviation — see below).
lessons.expected_effect: Livescan: 2-day sim of the 3% cap = +$7.6 net (fewer wide-spread reversal tails), no effect on the median grind. Sniper: near-zero effect at 10% (its edge lives at <1% spread), loose cap only trims pathological books.

lessons.trigger: BRISE #7928 (gateio→kucoin, expected +$0.615 → realized +$0.012, a 1.9% capture ratio) was labelled by the post-trade narrative as a 'clean, in-line settlement'.
lessons.problem: A post-trade narrative that classifies on sell-side price movement alone mislabels entry-side cost erosion as a clean fill — a settlement that captured 2% of its expected edge gets the same 'in-line' story as one that captured 95%, so the fund's public memory lies about why the edge vanished.
lessons.change: Narrative logic (no env): add expectation capture = realized/expected; new cause 'cost_drag' (small sell move AND capture < 0.5 → entry-side VWAP/slippage/fees ate the edge); 'in_line' now requires capture ≥ 0.6; capture ratio is stated in the prose and exposed in narrative.factors.capture_ratio.
lessons.expected_effect: Low-capture settlements with no adverse sell move are labelled 'cost_drag' with the capture % stated, instead of 'clean, in-line'. Every narrative now carries the capture number so no reader can mistake a 2% capture for a clean fill.

lessons.trigger: FOXY #7903 (bigspike_sniper_v1, gateio→kucoin, entry spread 0.955%, expected +$1.42 → realized −$14.93). The single largest drift tail of day one, on a spike name, at the full quiet size.
lessons.problem: On volatile spike names a full quiet-size position carries asymmetric 5-minute reversal risk — sizing to the footprint cap maximises exposure exactly in the regime where drift tails live, so the position is largest precisely where the hold is most dangerous.
lessons.change: NW_BIGSPIKE_SIZE_FACTOR: 1.0 (implicit) → 0.5 — halve the chosen quiet size on the sniper, respecting the SIZE_MIN_USD floor.
lessons.expected_effect: Roughly halves the magnitude of sniper reversal tails (a repeat of FOXY would settle near −$7.5, not −$14.9) at the cost of ~half the upside on winners. n=4 too small to tune precisely, so a conservative 0.5.
